zh.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt於1230年出生在瑞典斯奧艾林。她和Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1270年在丹麥維波格享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt于1230年出生在瑞典斯奧艾林。她和Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1270年在丹麦維波格享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Nielsen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Niels, Jorgen, Knud, Lars, Nels, Bent, Borge, Holger, Ove, Einer, Oluf.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Niels, a shortened form of Nikolaus (see Nicholas ). Compare Nielson , Neilsen , Nelsen , Nilsen , and Nilson .

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Neilsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Ejnar, Gurli, Jorgen, Nels, Niels.
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Nielsen  . Compare   Neilson   3.
Scottish (Midlothian): variant of   Neilson   1.

variants.name: Nelsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nels, Erik, Einer, Berger, Folmer, Holger, Iver, Juel, Morten, Thor, Uffe.
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Nielsen   or   Nilsen  . Compare   Nelson  .
Flemish and German: patronymic from   Nelis  .

nameMeanings.name: Hvide
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Claus, Eberhard, Hans, Juergen, Markus, Wolfgang.
German and Jewish: variant of   Heid  .
Dutch: rare variant of Van der Heide (see Vanderheide ) and, in North America, (also) a shortened form of this.

variants.name: Heidi
meanings: German: variant (and also an Americanized form, to retain the second syllable) of   Heide  .

variants.name: Heider
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Otto, Fritz, Gunter, Johann, Konrad, Kurt, Matthias, Willibald.

German and Swiss German: topographic name for a heath dweller, a variant of Heide + the -er suffix denoting an inhabitant; or a habitational name for someone from any of various places called Heide or Heidau (named with the same term); the placename is common in Silesia, Saxony, and Bohemia.

variants.name: Heid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Eckhart, Gerd, Gotthilf, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Kurt, Manfred, Ralf, Uli.
German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German heide, German Heide ‘heath, moor, open, uncultivated land’. Compare Heath .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Haido, a short form of any of various compound names beginning with haid ‘heath’ (i.e. wasteland, often with heather).

variants.name: Heyde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Achim, Dietrich, Helmut, Volker.
German: topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, unprotected, open country, a variant of   Heide  .

variants.name: Hiday
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Heide  .

nameMeanings.name: Galt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: variant of Gall , with excrescent -t.
English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English galte, gaute, gault ‘boar, hog’ (Old Norse gǫltr, galte), denoting someone thought to resemble the animal in appearance or character.

variants.name: Gault
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire), Irish (Antrim and Derry), and English: variant of   Galt  .
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Waldo, from wald ‘power, authority’.
French: habitational name from any of the places called Le Gault (for example in Loir-et-Cher and Eure-et-Loir), named with ancient Germanic walthu- ‘wood, forest’. Compare Legault .

variants.name: Gaut
meanings: English: variant of   Galt  .
In some cases possibly also French: variant of   Gault  .

variants.name: Gall
meanings: Scottish, Welsh, and Irish: nickname from Celtic gall ‘foreigner, stranger’, a word found in Irish, Gaelic, and Breton. In the Scottish Highlands the Gaelic term gall was applied to people from the English-speaking lowlands and to Scandinavians; in Ireland the same term was applied to settlers who arrived from Wales and England in the wake of the Anglo-Norman invasion of the 12th century. This surname is also found at an early date in Lincolnshire, where it apparently has a Breton origin (compare 4 below and Legall ), having been introduced by Breton followers of the Norman Conquerors.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Gale  .

French, German, Danish, Polish, and Czech; Slovak (mainly Gáll); Hungarian (Gáll): from the Latin personal name Gallus, originally a Roman surname meaning ‘Gaul’ or ‘rooster’ (see Gallo 2). The name was widespread in Europe during the Middle Ages, in central Europe largelly due to the cult of a 7th-century Irish monk and missionary, Saint Gall, whose name, Latinized as Gallus, is presumably of Celtic origin (see 1 above). Among other things, Saint Gall established a Christian settlement to the south of Lake Constance, which became the monastery later known as St. Gallen. The Latin(ized) name Gallus was taken into Czech as Havel , into Polish as Gaweł (see Gawel ), into Slovak and Hungarian as Gál (see Gal ), and into Slovenian and Croatian as Gal .

variants.name: Gatt
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): variant of Galt . An alternative derivation from Older Scots gate, gait, gaet, get(t) (Old Norse gata) ‘way, road, path; street’ (see Gates ) is theoretically possible, but there is no evidence to link this to any of the historical bearers of the name in the British Isles.
South German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed from the same root as the Middle High German word gate ‘companion, compatible person’.

nameMeanings.name: Ingerd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Ingegerd   and   Ingrid  , attested from the 16th-century.

nameMeanings.name: Margrete
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretha
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margarethe
meanings: from Greek. See Margaret .

historicEvents.description: King Eriksson died. Valdemar crowned King. Birger Jarl declared regent until Valdemar is of age.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

en.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt was born in 1230, in Ellinge, Skåne, Sweden. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. She died in 1270, in Viborg, Denmark, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt est née en 1230, à Ellinge, Skåne, Suède. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. Elle est décédée en 1270, à Viborg, Danemark, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt wurde 1230 in Ellinge, Skåne, Schweden geboren. Sie hatte mit Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1270 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Viborg, Dänemark.

it.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt è nata il nel 1230 nel Ellinge, Skåne, Svezia. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. Morì nel 1270, nel Viborg, Danimarca, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt(은)는 1230년 스웨덴 스ka니 엘링에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1270년 덴마크 비볼그에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt nasceu em 1230, em Ellinge, Skåne, Suécia. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. Ela faleceu em 1270, em Viborg, Dinamarca, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt родилась в 1230 г. в местности эллинг, скaн, Швеция. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. Она умерла в 1270 г. в местности виборг, Дания в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Ingerd Margrete Nielsen Hvide Galt nació en 1230, en Ellinge, Skåne, Suecia. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Ebbe Nielsen Hvide Galt. Murió en 1270, en Viborg, Dinamarca, a la edad de 40 años.
